name: Arthur Prysock
abstract: Arthur Prysock Jr. (January 1, 1924 or 1929 – June 21, 1997) was an American jazz and R&B singer best known for his live shows and his deep baritone, influenced by Billy Eckstine.

concerts.label: Sarah Vaughan and Her Trio | Arthur Prysock and His Group

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Mabern
about_quotes.evidence: I played with Arthur Prysock and I had to take off and I had Herbie Hancock to play Saturday night
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Mabern interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 31, 2010

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I used to play with him sometimes out at the Copa City in Long Island.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Duffy Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: So we go and we play the gig and it was with Arthur Prysock and everything, I mean 25,000 people and they were swinging, you know, groovin’.
about_quotes.doc_title: Duffy Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 17, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: I did "I Worry About You" with Arthur Prysock... I played drums on his first New York recording

about_quotes.speaker: Von Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been out there with Arthur Prysock and some more good singers that knocked me out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Von Freeman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee Sam Jones does not speak in the provided transcript; the interviewer mentions Arthur Prysock in the context of a band lineup, but there is no evidence of a personal or professional relationship between Sam Jones and Arthur Prysock.
